annotations.label: Topological space

anchor.snippet: A topological space is a set endowed with a structure, called a topology

annotations.note: The `TopologicalSpace` class endows a type with open sets satisfying the standard axioms.

annotations.label: Topological property

anchor.snippet: A property that is invariant under such deformations is a topological property

annotations.note: Mathlib has no decl capturing the meta-notion of a homeomorphism-invariant property as such.

annotations.label: Basic topological properties

anchor.snippet: the dimension , which allows distinguishing between a line and a surface

annotations.note: Dimension as a topological invariant is partially captured by `smallInductiveDimension`, but the general list of invariants is not a decl.

annotations.label: Seven Bridges of Königsberg

anchor.snippet: it was impossible to find a route through the town

annotations.note: Mathlib has the `IsEulerian` infrastructure but the actual Königsberg theorem (`Konigsberg.not_isEulerian`) lives in Archive, not Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem

anchor.snippet: the hairy ball theorem of algebraic topology says that

annotations.note: The hairy ball theorem appears only as an unproven title in docs/1000.yaml with no Mathlib decl.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism (intuitive)

anchor.snippet: two spaces are homeomorphic if one can be deformed into the other without cutting or gluing

annotations.note: `Homeomorph` is a continuous equivalence with continuous inverse, the rigorous form of this idea.

annotations.label: Coffee mug and doughnut

anchor.snippet: a topologist cannot distinguish a coffee mug from a doughnut

annotations.note: This informal illustration of a homeomorphism has no corresponding Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Homotopy equivalence

anchor.snippet: two objects are homotopy equivalent if they both result from

annotations.note: `ContinuousMap.HomotopyEquiv` is a pair of maps whose composites are homotopic to the identities.

annotations.label: Euler's polyhedron formula

anchor.snippet: This led to his polyhedron formula

annotations.note: The polyhedron formula V−E+F=2 appears only as an unproven title in docs/100.yaml with no decl.

annotations.label: Topology on a set

anchor.snippet: is called a topology on X if

annotations.note: `TopologicalSpace` is exactly a collection of open sets closed under the topology axioms.

annotations.label: Topological space (X, τ)

anchor.snippet: is called a topological space

annotations.note: A type with a `TopologicalSpace` instance is precisely the pair (X, τ).

annotations.label: Open set

anchor.snippet: are called open sets in X

annotations.note: `IsOpen s` is the predicate that `s` belongs to the topology.

annotations.label: Closed set

anchor.snippet: is said to be closed if its complement is in

annotations.note: `IsClosed s` is defined by `IsOpen sᶜ`, the complement being open.

annotations.label: Clopen set

anchor.snippet: both (a clopen set

annotations.note: `IsClopen s` is defined as `IsClosed s ∧ IsOpen s`.

annotations.label: Open neighborhood

anchor.snippet: is called an open neighborhood of

annotations.note: `OpenNhds x` is the type of open sets containing the point `x`.

annotations.label: Continuous function

anchor.snippet: called continuous if the inverse image of any open set is open

annotations.note: `Continuous f` requires the preimage of every open set to be open.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: the function is called a homeomorphism and the domain of the function is said to be homeomorphic to the range

annotations.note: `Homeomorph` is a bijection that is continuous with continuous inverse.

annotations.label: Cube and sphere homeomorphic

anchor.snippet: The cube and the sphere are homeomorphic, as are the coffee cup and the doughnut

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl constructs a homeomorphism between a cube and a sphere.

annotations.label: Sphere not homeomorphic to doughnut

anchor.snippet: the sphere is not homeomorphic to the doughnut

annotations.note: Mathlib has no result distinguishing the 2-sphere from the torus up to homeomorphism.

annotations.label: Manifold

anchor.snippet: A manifold is a topological space that resembles Euclidean space near each point

annotations.note: `ChartedSpace H M` formalizes a space locally modeled on `H`, generalizing the Euclidean model space.

annotations.label: One-dimensional manifolds

anchor.snippet: Lines and circles , but not figure eights , are one-dimensional manifolds

annotations.note: This illustrative contrast of 1-manifolds versus a figure eight has no dedicated Mathlib decl.

annotations.label: Surface (2-manifold)

anchor.snippet: Two-dimensional manifolds are also called surfaces

annotations.note: Surfaces are expressible as `ChartedSpace` over a 2-dimensional model, but there is no dedicated `Surface` definition.

annotations.label: Surface examples

anchor.snippet: Examples include the plane , the sphere , and the torus

annotations.note: The sphere and plane are formalized as manifolds via `ChartedSpace`, but this list of surface examples is not itself a decl.

annotations.label: General topology

anchor.snippet: General topology is the branch of topology dealing with the basic set-theoretic definitions

annotations.note: This names a subfield of mathematics rather than a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Compact set

anchor.snippet: Compact sets are those that can be covered by finitely many sets of arbitrarily small size

annotations.note: `IsCompact` formalizes compactness (every open cover has a finite subcover) via filters.

annotations.label: Connected set

anchor.snippet: Connected sets are sets that cannot be divided into two pieces that are far apart

annotations.note: `IsConnected` captures that a nonempty set admits no separation into two disjoint open pieces.

annotations.label: Metric space

anchor.snippet: Metric spaces are an important class of topological spaces where the distance between any two points is defined by a function called a metric

annotations.note: `MetricSpace` endows a type with a distance function satisfying the metric axioms.

annotations.label: Open set in a metric space

anchor.snippet: an open set is a union of open disks

annotations.note: `Metric.isOpen_iff` characterizes open sets as those containing a ball around each point.

annotations.label: Algebraic topology

anchor.snippet: Algebraic topology is a branch of mathematics that uses tools from algebra to study topological spaces

annotations.note: This names a subfield rather than a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Subgroup of a free group is free

anchor.snippet: any subgroup of a free group is again a free group

annotations.note: The Nielsen–Schreier theorem is the instance `subgroupIsFreeOfIsFree`.

annotations.label: Differential topology

anchor.snippet: Differential topology is the field dealing with differentiable functions on differentiable manifolds

annotations.note: This names a subfield rather than a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Geometric topology

anchor.snippet: Geometric topology is a branch of topology that primarily focuses on low-dimensional manifolds

annotations.note: This names a subfield rather than a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Uniformization theorem

anchor.snippet: every surface admits a constant curvature metric

annotations.note: The uniformization theorem appears only as an unproven title in docs/1000.yaml with no decl.

annotations.label: Geometrization theorem

anchor.snippet: every 3-manifold can be cut into pieces, each of which has one of eight possible geometries

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration formalizes Thurston's geometrization theorem.

annotations.label: Pointless topology

anchor.snippet: In pointless topology one considers instead the lattice of open sets as the basic notion of the theory

annotations.note: The lattice-of-opens abstraction is formalized as `Order.Frame` (locales), but pointless topology as a field is not a single decl.

annotations.label: Grothendieck topology

anchor.snippet: Grothendieck topologies are structures defined on arbitrary categories that allow the definition of sheaves on those categories

annotations.note: `GrothendieckTopology` is defined as a structure assigning sieves to objects of a category.

annotations.label: Topological data analysis

anchor.snippet: Topological data analysis uses techniques from algebraic topology to determine the large-scale structure of a set

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration formalizes topological data analysis or persistent homology.

annotations.label: Topological quantum field theory

anchor.snippet: A topological quantum field theory (or topological field theory or TQFT) is a quantum field theory that computes topological invariants

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration defines a topological quantum field theory.

annotations.label: Configuration space

anchor.snippet: The possible positions of a robot can be described by a manifold called configuration space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no robotics configuration-space manifold definition (only an unrelated symbolic-dynamics usage of the term).
